



Situations that deem a raise:



Large increase in responsibilities (changes officially made to 4R document)



Move into management position or overseeing of team in some way 



Cost-of-living increase over time (yearly)



Frequency and Percentage of Raises:



We do not give raises based on any sort of preset cadence nor are raises equal within the company or departments. Raises are based on a case by case and are dependent on the situations above. All raises must be approved by CFO/CEO.  Salaries and hourly wages should be reviewed on an annual basis based on hire date or last pay increase date.  No more than one pay raise should be given in a 12 month period without special circumstances. 



Common times raises are given: 



Promotion (not always)



Increase in direct reports (move to management or higher level management)



Increase in responsibility (must be approved by executive supervisor)



What to Expect as an Amount for Your Raise:



Consistent Performance and Reliability = 2-3%



Added Responsibilities 5%



Promotion to Management/New Skill Acquisition/immense increase in responsibilities = 5-10%+
